Plot natal dispersal events over time

Source: R/visualization.R
plot_dispersal_events.Rd

Draws the count of natal dispersal moves per tick as a bar chart overlaid with a smoothed trend line. Each bar represents the number of agents that moved away from their birthplace during that tick. When dispersal_evolution = FALSE all bars are zero.

Usage

plot_dispersal_events(run_data)

Arguments

run_data

A list returned by get_run_data(). Must contain a $ticks data frame with columns t and n_dispersal_events.

Value

A ggplot2::ggplot() object, or NULL invisibly when the dispersal column is absent.

Details

Plot natal dispersal events over time

See also

plot_run(), get_run_data()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
specs <- default_specs(); specs$dispersal_evolution <- TRUE
env   <- run_clade(specs)
data  <- get_run_data(env)
plot_dispersal_events(data)
} # }
